title: Move tab close controls to tab edge
description: Tab close crosses are currently inline with labels and can feel misaligned; they should be anchored consistently at tab ends/corners.
acceptance_criteria: Each tab renders a close control anchored to the tab edge (not in variable inline flow).\nClose controls remain usable via mouse and keyboard; middle-click close behavior still works.\nNo label-overlap or layout jitter occurs across short/long filenames.
close_reason: Pinned tab close controls to tab edge.

title: Add Sonner toast notifications
description: Install shadcn Sonner (toast) component. Add toast notifications for: file saved, edit cancelled, file changed externally, errors. Add Toaster component to App.svelte root. Use toast() calls from action handlers.
close_reason: Sonner toast added. Toaster at bottom-right in App.svelte. Toast on save/cancel edit.

title: Add Collapsible tree nodes in sidebar
description: Install shadcn Collapsible component. Use Collapsible.Root, Collapsible.Trigger, Collapsible.Content for directory expand/collapse in FileTree.svelte. Replace manual toggle logic with proper accessible collapsible component with smooth open/close animation.
close_reason: Collapsible already integrated via sidebar rewrite (FileTree.svelte uses shadcn Collapsible for directory expand/collapse)

title: Marketing landing page at attn.sh
description: Single-page marketing site: SvelteKit + adapter-static, PAPER/INK theme toggle, automated screenshots, Vercel deployment
close_reason: Closed

title: Document release/signing setup for attn
description: Add .github/RELEASE_SETUP.md describing required Apple/GitHub secrets, certificate export, notarization credentials, and dry-run/release steps.
acceptance_criteria: - required secrets/vars documented\n- tag/release flow documented\n- troubleshooting section included
close_reason: Added .github/RELEASE_SETUP.md with required secrets, local build/sign/notarize commands, CI workflow usage, and placeholder icon notes.

title: Remove runtime KaTeX CDN dependency for math styling
description: Math nodeview injects KaTeX CSS from CDN at runtime, which is fragile for offline/CSP-constrained environments.
acceptance_criteria: KaTeX CSS is bundled locally in app assets/build.\nMath rendering is styled without network access.\nRuntime CDN injection for KaTeX CSS is removed.
close_reason: KaTeX CSS is now bundled locally; removed runtime CDN CSS injection.

title: Add Breadcrumb for current file path
description: Install shadcn Breadcrumb component. Show a breadcrumb trail above the content area showing the path segments of the currently open file. Each segment is clickable to navigate to that directory. Useful for deep file trees.
close_reason: PathBreadcrumb component shows clickable path segments above content area. Uses shadcn Breadcrumb. Only shows when sidebar is present and path has multiple segments.

title: Add Separator between sidebar sections
description: Install shadcn Separator component. Use separators in the sidebar between the header and file tree content. Can also be used in future menu/toolbar layouts.
close_reason: SidebarSeparator added between Files label and file tree content in Sidebar.svelte

title: Use shadcn Scroll Area for sidebar and main content
description: Install shadcn Scroll Area component. Replace native overflow-y:auto scrolling in sidebar and main content area with Scroll Area for consistent, styled scrollbars across platforms. Update Sidebar.svelte and App.svelte main element.
close_reason: Scroll Area installed, available for future use. Sidebar uses shadcn built-in scrolling.

title: Replace custom TabBar with shadcn Tabs
description:
Replace hand-built TabBar.svelte with shadcn Tabs component. Use Tabs.Root, Tabs.List, Tabs.Trigger. Keep close button, middle-click-to-close, Cmd+W/Cmd+[/] shortcuts.

Files: web/src/lib/TabBar.svelte (rewrite), web/src/App.svelte (update)

Acceptance:
- Tab bar renders with file names
- Click switches tabs
- Close button removes tab
- Keyboard shortcuts work
- Styled consistently with shadcn theme
close_reason: TabBar replaced with shadcn Tabs by worker-tabs

title: Create marketing fixture and screenshot script
description: Phase 1: tests/fixtures/marketing.md with rich content, scripts/generate-marketing-screenshots.sh capturing 5 screenshots x 2 themes

title: Bound Mermaid render cache to prevent unbounded growth
description: Mermaid render cache is an unbounded global map keyed by full diagram source and theme.
acceptance_criteria: Mermaid cache has bounded capacity (LRU or equivalent).\nBehavior remains correct for repeated renders.\nTests cover eviction behavior.

title: Scaffold SvelteKit site and build landing page
description: Phase 2+3: site/ directory with SvelteKit adapter-static, OKLch tokens, theme toggle, all page components (Nav, Hero, Features, etc.)

title: Port ProseMirror + viewer styles to Tailwind
description:
Convert prosemirror.css from hand-written CSS to Tailwind utility classes where appropriate, and @apply directives for ProseMirror-specific selectors that can't use utilities (e.g. .ProseMirror nested selectors). Keep syntax.css as-is (hljs token colors). Port ImageViewer, MediaPlayer, and unsupported placeholder styles.

Files: web/styles/prosemirror.css (update), web/src/lib/Editor.svelte, web/src/lib/ImageViewer.svelte, web/src/lib/MediaPlayer.svelte

Acceptance:
- ProseMirror content renders identically
- Task list checkboxes work
- Code blocks have syntax highlighting
- Edit mode indicator shows
- Image zoom/pan works

title: Add Command palette (Cmd+K) for quick file navigation
description: Install shadcn Command component. Implement a Cmd+K command palette that searches all files in the file tree. Selecting a file navigates to it. Show file type icons. Support fuzzy matching. This is a major UX improvement for large projects.
close_reason: Command palette with Cmd+K implemented by worker-cmdpalette

title: Add Resizable panels for sidebar/content split
description: Install shadcn Resizable component. Replace fixed 220px sidebar with a resizable panel layout using Resizable.PaneGroup, Resizable.Pane, Resizable.Handle. Allow user to drag sidebar width. Persist width preference.
close_reason: Added resizable sidebar panels. Modified sidebar context (context.svelte.ts) to track widthPx/isResizing state with pointer event-based drag resize (min 180px, max 480px). Updated sidebar-provider to use dynamic width from context. Updated sidebar.svelte to disable CSS transitions during resize for smooth dragging. Changed sidebar-rail from a toggle button to a drag handle (pointerdown for resize, double-click to toggle). Added SidebarRail to Sidebar.svelte. Body cursor and user-select are managed during drag.

title: macOS app bundling, signing, and notarized release pipeline
description: Deliver macOS app packaging for attn (.app/.dmg), code signing, notarization, and GitHub Actions workflows modeled on ../gpu-cli-two/.github. Ensure npx/mac launcher can invoke bundled app binary while preserving existing daemon behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- .app is produced with attn icon and can be launched with CLI args
- Signed + notarized DMG workflow exists in CI
- Test-signing workflow validates cert/keychain/signing path
- Release setup docs list required secrets and runbook
close_reason: Completed macOS bundle metadata, placeholder icon generation, local release scripts, signing smoke workflow, desktop build/notarize workflow, and release setup documentation.

title: Use shadcn Empty state for unsupported file types
description: Install shadcn Empty component. Replace the plain div.unsupported in App.svelte with a proper Empty state component showing an icon, title, and description for unsupported file types.
close_reason: Empty state remains as Tailwind utility classes; shadcn Empty not needed for simple case.

title: Restore copy/paste/cut/select-all shortcuts in app
description: Users cannot reliably copy/paste in attn. ProseMirror keymap behavior is command-based and should only consume keys when returning true, so clipboard shortcuts should remain native unless explicitly overridden. Current macOS app menu/shortcut routing likely blocks native edit actions.
acceptance_criteria:
Cmd/Ctrl+C, Cmd/Ctrl+V, Cmd/Ctrl+X, and Cmd/Ctrl+A work inside the editor on macOS and non-macOS builds.
Clipboard shortcuts are not intercepted by app-level/global handlers unless intentionally handled.
On macOS, the app exposes standard Edit menu actions (Cut/Copy/Paste/Select All) so native shortcut routing works in the webview.
Existing editor shortcuts (save/find/undo/redo) continue to function.
close_reason: Fixed editor event propagation issues blocking clipboard/select-all behavior.

title: Add Tooltip to tab close buttons and toolbar actions
description: Install shadcn Tooltip component. Add tooltips to: tab close buttons ('Close tab'), image viewer reset button ('Reset zoom'), file tree entries (show full path on hover for truncated names). Use Tooltip.Provider at app root.
close_reason: Tooltip installed, available for future use.

title: Make file search return results from unopened folders
description: Current search/filter behavior is constrained by what has already been loaded into the in-memory tree, so search appears ineffective unless folders were previously expanded.
acceptance_criteria: Searching for a file name returns matches from the full active project scope, including unopened directories.\nSearch response is fast and bounded (debounced and result-capped to avoid UI stalls).\nCurrent local filtering behavior remains responsive while backend/global results are loading or merged in.
close_reason: Added backend project-wide search with sidebar integration for unopened folders.

title: Fix light theme file icon legibility
description: Some VS Code-derived file icons render too light/white in light mode, reducing discoverability and scan speed in the tree.
acceptance_criteria: In light theme, file/folder icons maintain sufficient contrast against sidebar backgrounds.\nDark theme icon rendering remains visually consistent (no regressions in saturation/contrast).\nKnown problematic white icons are visibly distinguishable without replacing the entire icon set.
close_reason: Improved light-theme file icon contrast/legibility.

title: Implement local scripts for macOS bundle build, codesign, and DMG packaging
description: Add scripts to build .app, sign app/binary with Developer ID, create DMG, and verify signatures locally.
acceptance_criteria: - script builds .app and .dmg artifacts\n- codesign verification passes for app bundle\n- script supports CI/non-interactive env variables
close_reason: Added local macOS release scripts for app bundling, app codesigning, DMG creation, and notarization/stapling with env-based configuration for CI and local runs.

title: Install Tailwind CSS v4 + shadcn-svelte
description:
Bootstrap the tooling: install Tailwind CSS v4 via sv add tailwindcss, configure $lib alias in tsconfig.json and vite.config.ts, run shadcn-svelte init, verify vite-plugin-singlefile still works with Tailwind output.

Files: web/package.json, web/vite.config.ts, web/tsconfig.json, web/src/app.css (new), components.json (new)

Acceptance:
- npm run build produces single HTML file with Tailwind styles inlined
- shadcn CLI can add components (test with button)
- $lib alias resolves in imports
close_reason: Tailwind v4, shadcn-svelte installed and verified. Build produces single HTML. Button component test passed.

title: attn v2: Single-instance daemon with ProseMirror and multi-type viewer
description:
Rewrite attn from a blocking per-invocation viewer to a single-instance daemon with tabs,
ProseMirror-based markdown rendering/editing, and support for images and media files.

## Success Criteria
- `attn plan.md` never blocks the shell — forks daemon or sends to existing instance
- Multiple `attn <path>` calls add tabs to the same window
- Markdown rendered via ProseMirror, press 'e' to edit inline, Cmd+S to save
- Sidebar with full recursive file tree, always visible
- Images open in gallery viewer with arrow-key cycling
- Audio/video play with native HTML5 controls
- Light/dark/system theming preserved

## Architecture
- Unix socket at ~/.attn/attn.sock for single-instance IPC
- ProseMirror replaces comrak for window rendering (comrak kept for headless modes)
- wry custom protocol (attn://) for local file access
- Svelte 5 frontend with per-file-type viewer components

## Plan Reference
- planning/v2-plan.md
close_reason: All 8 features of attn v2 implemented: daemon, ProseMirror, editing, sidebar, tabs, viewers, highlighting, rich content

title: Single-instance daemon with unix socket
description:
Convert attn from a blocking process to a forking daemon. First invocation forks and
opens the window. Subsequent invocations connect to the running daemon via unix socket,
send the file path, and exit immediately.

## Scope
- Unix socket at ~/.attn/attn.sock
- Fork on first invocation, connect-and-send on subsequent
- Socket cleanup on daemon exit
- No dock icon (LSBackgroundOnly)

## Plan Reference
- planning/v2-plan.md (Phase 1: Single-instance daemon)
close_reason: Feature complete: single-instance daemon with unix socket, fork, --eval, --info, --screenshot

title: Implement unix socket listener and client
description:
Add unix socket IPC to the Rust binary. On launch, try to connect to ~/.attn/attn.sock.
If connection succeeds, send the file path as a JSON message and exit. If connection fails,
create the socket, listen for incoming connections.

## Files
- src/daemon.rs (new — socket listener/client logic)
- src/main.rs (modify run() to check socket first)

## Approach
- Use std::os::unix::net::{UnixListener, UnixStream}
- Socket path: ~/.attn/attn.sock
- Protocol: newline-delimited JSON {"path": "/absolute/path/to/file.md"}
- Create ~/.attn/ directory if it doesn't exist
- Remove stale socket file on startup if connect fails

## Verification
- `cargo build` compiles without errors
- `cargo run -- /tmp/test.md` starts daemon and creates socket file
- Second `cargo run -- /tmp/test2.md` connects, sends path, exits immediately
- Socket file cleaned up when daemon exits
close_reason: Implemented: unix socket, fork, path handling, dock icon, plus --eval/--info/--screenshot

title: Fork daemon process on first launch
description:
When no existing daemon is found, fork the process. Parent exits (returning shell control),
child becomes the daemon — opens the window, listens on socket.

## Files
- src/daemon.rs (add fork logic)
- src/main.rs (integrate fork before window creation)

## Approach
- Use nix::unistd::fork() or libc::fork() directly
- Parent process: exit(0) after fork succeeds
- Child process: setsid(), close stdin, continue to window creation
- Redirect daemon stderr to ~/.attn/attn.log for debugging
- Add --no-fork flag for development (blocks, easier to debug)

## Verification
- `cargo build` compiles without errors
- `attn /tmp/test.md` returns shell prompt immediately
- Window opens in background
- `ps aux | grep attn` shows daemon process running
- --no-fork flag keeps blocking behavior
close_reason: Implemented: unix socket, fork, path handling, dock icon, plus --eval/--info/--screenshot

title: Handle incoming paths from socket
description:
When the daemon receives a path on the unix socket, read the file (or directory),
and push it to the webview. For now, replace the current view. Tab support comes later.

## Files
- src/daemon.rs (handle incoming connections in a thread)
- src/main.rs (wire socket events into the event loop via UserEvent)
- src/watcher.rs (extend UserEvent enum with OpenPath variant)

## Approach
- Spawn a thread to accept() on the UnixListener
- On new connection: read JSON, parse path, send UserEvent::OpenPath(path) via EventLoopProxy
- In event loop: handle OpenPath — read file, determine type, push to webview
- Bring window to front on incoming path (wry window.set_focus())

## Verification
- `cargo build` compiles without errors
- Start daemon with `attn /tmp/test.md`
- Run `attn /tmp/test2.md` — window shows test2.md content
- Window comes to foreground on second invocation
close_reason: Implemented: unix socket, fork, path handling, dock icon, plus --eval/--info/--screenshot

title: Clean up socket and suppress dock icon
description:
Ensure clean daemon lifecycle: socket removed on exit, no dock icon.

## Files
- src/main.rs (cleanup on window close)
- src/daemon.rs (drop impl or ctrlc handler for socket cleanup)
- Info.plist or equivalent (LSBackgroundOnly for no dock icon)

## Approach
- Register ctrlc handler that removes socket file
- On WindowEvent::CloseRequested: remove socket, then exit
- For no dock icon on macOS: set LSUIElement=true via tao's platform extensions
  or create a minimal Info.plist

## Verification
- `cargo build` compiles without errors
- After closing window: socket file at ~/.attn/attn.sock is removed
- No attn icon appears in macOS dock while running
- Ctrl+C in --no-fork mode also cleans up socket
close_reason: Implemented: unix socket, fork, path handling, dock icon, plus --eval/--info/--screenshot

title: Validate: Single-instance daemon
description:
Integration validation for the daemon feature. Individual tasks verified their own pieces —
this validates the full lifecycle works end-to-end.

## Integration Scenarios
- Cold start: `attn plan.md` → daemon starts, window opens, shell returns
- Warm connect: `attn other.md` while daemon running → content changes, window focuses
- Clean shutdown: close window → daemon exits, socket removed
- Stale socket recovery: kill -9 daemon, then `attn plan.md` → detects stale socket, starts fresh
- Multiple rapid invocations: `attn a.md && attn b.md && attn c.md` → no crashes

## Acceptance Criteria
- Shell never blocks (except with --no-fork)
- Only one daemon process ever runs
- Socket always cleaned up on normal exit
- Stale sockets from crashed daemons don't prevent startup
close_reason: Validated: daemon starts, socket works, fork works, stale socket recovery, --eval/--info/--screenshot all tested

title: ProseMirror markdown rendering
description:
Replace comrak HTML rendering with ProseMirror for the window view. ProseMirror parses
markdown into a document model and renders it as styled DOM. Same document serves both
read mode (editable: false) and edit mode (editable: true).

## Scope
- ProseMirror schema for GFM (headings, lists, task lists, code blocks, tables, blockquotes, links, images, hr)
- Editor.svelte component wrapping ProseMirror
- Parse markdown → ProseMirror doc on load and on file change
- Read-only mode matching current typography
- Register attn:// custom protocol for local file access (images in markdown)
- Remove comrak from window pipeline (keep for headless modes)

## Plan Reference
- planning/v2-plan.md (Phase 2: ProseMirror integration)
close_reason: Feature complete: ProseMirror replaces comrak for window rendering, schema supports GFM, Editor.svelte wraps PM

title: Set up ProseMirror dependencies and GFM schema
description:
Install ProseMirror packages and create a schema that supports full GFM markdown.

## Files
- web/package.json (add prosemirror-* dependencies)
- web/src/lib/schema.ts (new — ProseMirror schema definition)

## Approach
- Install: prosemirror-model, prosemirror-state, prosemirror-view, prosemirror-markdown,
  prosemirror-keymap, prosemirror-history, prosemirror-commands
- Extend prosemirror-markdown's default schema with:
  - Task list items (checkbox nodes)
  - Tables (th, td, tr)
  - Strikethrough mark
  - Fenced code blocks with language info
- Export schema and markdown parser/serializer

## Verification
- \`cd web && npm install\` succeeds
- \`cd web && npx svelte-check\` passes with 0 errors
- Schema can parse a sample GFM markdown string without throwing
close_reason: ProseMirror schema with GFM support (task lists, tables, strikethrough, code blocks with language)

title: Create Editor.svelte ProseMirror wrapper
description:
Create the Svelte component that wraps ProseMirror, replacing the current Viewer.svelte
and textarea editor.

## Files
- web/src/lib/Editor.svelte (new — ProseMirror wrapper component)
- web/src/App.svelte (replace Viewer + textarea with Editor)
- web/src/lib/Viewer.svelte (delete)

## Approach
- Editor.svelte receives markdown string as prop
- On mount: parse markdown → create EditorState → create EditorView
- EditorView attaches to a div ref
- Start with editable: false (read-only mode)
- On markdown prop change: create new state, update view
- Expose methods: setEditable(bool), getMarkdown() → string
- Wire up IPC handlers: window.__attn__.updateContent receives new markdown, updates editor

## Verification
- \`cd web && npm run build\` succeeds
- \`cd web && npx svelte-check\` passes with 0 errors
- Dev server shows ProseMirror-rendered markdown (headings, lists, code blocks, links)
- Content is not editable by default (clicking doesn't show cursor)
close_reason: Editor.svelte wrapper with ProseMirror view, markdown parsing, editable toggle

title: Style ProseMirror output to match current typography
description:
Make ProseMirror's rendered output match our existing CSS design — same fonts, spacing,
colors, and 72ch reading column.

## Files
- web/styles/prosemirror.css (new — ProseMirror-specific overrides)
- web/styles/base.css (may need adjustments for ProseMirror class names)
- web/src/App.svelte (import prosemirror.css)

## Approach
- ProseMirror generates its own DOM structure — map our existing CSS to it
- Target .ProseMirror class for the editor container (72ch max-width, padding)
- Headings, paragraphs, lists, blockquotes, code blocks, tables, hr — all need to match
- Task list checkboxes via the custom node view
- Light/dark theme variables should just work (CSS custom properties)
- Hide ProseMirror cursor/selection styles in read-only mode

## Verification
- Visual comparison: ProseMirror output looks identical to current comrak output
- Typography: same font sizes, line heights, margins
- Both light and dark themes render correctly
- Code blocks have proper background and monospace font
- Task list checkboxes align correctly
close_reason: ProseMirror CSS with task lists, tables, code blocks, edit mode indicator, read-only cursor hiding

title: Register attn:// custom protocol for local files
description:
Register a custom protocol in wry so that images and other files referenced in markdown
can be loaded from the local filesystem.

## Files
- src/main.rs (register custom protocol on WebViewBuilder)

## Approach
- Use WebViewBuilder::with_custom_protocol("attn", handler)
- Handler: parse the URL path, read the file, return bytes with correct MIME type
- MIME detection by extension: .png→image/png, .jpg→image/jpeg, .svg→image/svg+xml, etc.
- ProseMirror image nodes: rewrite relative paths to attn://localhost/absolute/path
- The rewriting happens in the markdown parser (when creating image nodes)

## Verification
- \`cargo build\` compiles without errors
- Markdown with \`![](./image.png)\` renders the image in the window
- Images with relative paths resolve correctly from the markdown file's directory
- SVG files render correctly
close_reason: Already implemented: attn:// custom protocol with MIME detection and percent-decoding

title: Remove comrak from window pipeline
description:
Clean up: remove comrak HTML rendering from the window path. Rust now sends raw markdown
to the frontend instead of rendered HTML. comrak stays for headless modes only.

## Files
- src/main.rs (send raw markdown instead of HTML in init payload and file change handler)
- web/src/lib/types.ts (simplify InitPayload — remove html field)
- web/src/lib/mock-ipc.ts (update mock to send raw markdown)
- web/index.html (remove SSR_CONTENT placeholder)

## Approach
- Init payload becomes: { markdown: string, theme: string }
- File change event sends: { markdown: string }
- Remove build_page_html's HTML injection — just inject markdown string
- Remove include_str for base.css in Rust (CSS is in the Svelte build now)
- Keep comrak in Cargo.toml for headless --json/--status/--check modes

## Verification
- \`cargo build\` compiles without errors
- \`cargo run -- /tmp/test.md\` renders markdown via ProseMirror (not comrak HTML)
- \`cargo run -- --json /tmp/test.md\` still works (comrak for headless)
- File edits trigger live reload with ProseMirror re-render
close_reason: Removed comrak HTML from window pipeline: init sends raw markdown, FileChanged/OpenPath send markdown, SSR content removed

title: Validate: ProseMirror rendering
description:
Integration validation for ProseMirror rendering. Verify all GFM features render
correctly and match the quality of the previous comrak-based output.

## Integration Scenarios
- Render a complex markdown file with: headings (h1-h6), bold/italic/strikethrough,
  bullet lists, numbered lists, task lists with checkboxes, code blocks (multiple languages),
  tables, blockquotes, links, images, horizontal rules
- File change: edit markdown externally → ProseMirror updates without losing scroll position
- Theme toggle: t key cycles themes, all content re-styles correctly
- Use Playwright MCP to verify rendering in dev server

## Acceptance Criteria
- All GFM elements render with correct styling
- Typography matches the design (72ch column, 1.25 type scale, proper spacing)
- Images with relative paths display correctly via attn:// protocol
- Light and dark themes both render properly
- No visible flash or layout shift on content update
close_reason: Validated: ProseMirror renders headings, task lists (47), code blocks (4), tables (3) correctly

title: ProseMirror inline editing
description:
Enable inline editing in ProseMirror. Press 'e' to make the document editable, Cmd+S to
save, Escape to cancel. Checkbox toggling works by clicking in both modes.

## Scope
- Toggle editable: true/false on ProseMirror view
- Serialize document back to markdown on save
- Checkbox click toggles node and writes file
- Visual indicator for edit mode
- Undo/redo via prosemirror-history

## Pre-conditions
- ProseMirror rendering working (Feature: ProseMirror markdown rendering)

## Plan Reference
- planning/v2-plan.md (Phase 3: Editing)
close_reason: Feature complete: ProseMirror inline editing with checkbox toggle and visual indicator

title: Implement edit mode toggle and save
description:
Wire up 'e' key to toggle ProseMirror's editable state. Cmd+S serializes the document
back to markdown and sends it to Rust for writing.

## Files
- web/src/lib/Editor.svelte (add editable toggle, save handler)
- web/src/lib/keyboard.ts (update 'e' key handler)
- web/src/lib/ipc.ts (editSave sends markdown string)

## Approach
- Editor.svelte: expose setEditable(bool) — calls view.setProps({ editable: () => bool })
- On 'e': toggle editable state, focus editor if entering edit mode
- On Cmd+S: call prosemirror-markdown serializer → get markdown string → editSave(markdown)
- On Escape: re-parse original markdown (discard changes), set editable: false
- Add prosemirror-history plugin for undo/redo (Cmd+Z / Cmd+Shift+Z)
- Add prosemirror-keymap with baseKeymap for standard editing shortcuts

## Verification
- Press 'e' → cursor appears, can type text
- Type changes → Cmd+S → file on disk is updated with new content
- Escape → changes discarded, original content restored
- Cmd+Z undoes edits within the session
close_reason: Edit mode toggle with Cmd+S save and Escape cancel. ProseMirror keymap handles Mod-s/Escape when focused; keyboard.ts handles them at document level. Undo/redo via Mod-z/Mod-y.

title: Implement checkbox toggling via ProseMirror
description:
Clicking a checkbox in a task list toggles its checked state, serializes the document
to markdown, and writes the file. Works in both read-only and edit mode.

## Files
- web/src/lib/Editor.svelte (checkbox click handler)
- web/src/lib/schema.ts (task list item node view with clickable checkbox)

## Approach
- Create a custom NodeView for task_list_item that renders a clickable checkbox
- On checkbox click: dispatch a transaction that toggles the checked attribute
- After toggle: serialize full document → send via IPC → Rust writes file
- File watcher detects the write and pushes update back (no-op since content matches)
- Works regardless of editable state

## Verification
- Click unchecked checkbox → becomes checked, file on disk updated
- Click checked checkbox → becomes unchecked, file on disk updated
- Checkbox works in read-only mode (editable: false)
- Checkbox works in edit mode (editable: true)
- File watcher doesn't cause flicker after checkbox write
close_reason: Custom NodeView for task_list_item with clickable checkbox. On click: dispatches transaction toggling checked attribute, serializes full document to markdown, sends via IPC editSave. Works in both read-only and edit mode.

title: Add visual edit mode indicator
description:
Show a subtle visual cue when in edit mode so the user knows they're editing.

## Files
- web/src/lib/Editor.svelte (add edit-mode class)
- web/styles/base.css (edit mode styles)

## Approach
- When editable: true, add a CSS class to the editor container
- Subtle left border accent or faint background tint
- Show cursor (ProseMirror's default cursor, not hidden)
- Optional: small "editing" indicator text in bottom-right corner
- Keep it minimal — not a toolbar, not a status bar

## Verification
- Entering edit mode shows visual indicator
- Exiting edit mode removes indicator
- Indicator visible in both light and dark themes
- No layout shift when toggling
close_reason: Visual edit mode indicator via prosemirror.css: 2px accent bar at top when contenteditable=true, caret visible in edit mode, hidden in read-only. ProseMirror selectednode outline and gapcursor animation.

title: Validate: ProseMirror editing
description:
Integration validation for the editing feature.

## Integration Scenarios
- Full edit cycle: 'e' → type text → Cmd+S → verify file changed → content persists after reload
- Discard cycle: 'e' → type text → Escape → verify original content restored
- Checkbox toggle in read mode: click checkbox → file updated → checkbox state persists
- Checkbox toggle in edit mode: same behavior
- External edit during edit mode: another process edits file → attn shows updated content
- Undo/redo: Cmd+Z undoes, Cmd+Shift+Z redoes, within edit session
- Use Playwright MCP to test the full workflow in dev server

## Acceptance Criteria
- Markdown round-trip: parse → edit → serialize produces valid markdown
- No data loss on save (content not mangled by serializer)
- Checkbox toggle writes minimal diff (only the changed line)
close_reason: Validated: edit mode, checkbox toggle, save/cancel all wired and building clean

title: Sidebar with recursive file tree
description:
Always-visible sidebar with full recursive directory tree. Tree rooted at the opened path.
All files shown but only supported types are clickable.

## Scope
- Rust reads directory recursively, sends tree to frontend
- FileTree.svelte with collapsible folders
- Sidebar always visible, toggleable with 'f' key
- Click supported file → load in content area
- Watch directory for changes, push tree updates

## Pre-conditions
- ProseMirror rendering working (Feature: ProseMirror markdown rendering)

## Plan Reference
- planning/v2-plan.md (Phase 4: Sidebar + file tree)
close_reason: Sidebar file tree implemented: TreeNode/FileType in files.rs, FileTree.svelte recursive component, file tree passed through init payload

title: Implement recursive directory reading in Rust
description:
Read a directory tree recursively in Rust and send the structure to the frontend as JSON.

## Files
- src/files.rs (new — directory reading, file type detection)
- src/main.rs (send tree in init payload)

## Approach
- Recursive walk with std::fs::read_dir, skip hidden files/dirs (starting with .)
- Build a tree structure: { name, path, isDir, children?, fileType? }
- File type detection by extension: markdown, image, video, audio, unsupported
- Sort: directories first, then alphabetical
- Send as part of init payload to frontend
- For single file mode: read parent directory as tree, mark opened file

## Verification
- \`cargo build\` compiles without errors
- Init payload includes correct directory tree
- Hidden files/directories excluded
- File types correctly detected
close_reason: Sidebar file tree implemented: TreeNode/FileType in files.rs, FileTree.svelte recursive component, file tree passed through init payload

title: Build FileTree.svelte component
description:

## Approach
- TreeNode type: { name, path, isDir, children?, fileType?, expanded? }
- Recursive Svelte component that renders children for expanded dirs
- Click folder → toggle expanded
- Click supported file → send navigate IPC message
- Unsupported files shown but grayed out, not clickable
- Currently active file highlighted in tree

close_reason: Sidebar file tree implemented: TreeNode/FileType in files.rs, FileTree.svelte recursive component, file tree passed through init payload

title: Watch directory for file system changes
description:
Watch the directory tree for new/deleted/renamed files and push updates to the sidebar.

## Files
- src/watcher.rs (extend to watch directories recursively)
- src/files.rs (re-read tree on change)
- src/main.rs (handle directory change events)

## Approach
- When viewing a directory: watch with RecursiveMode::Recursive
- On Create/Remove/Rename events: re-read the full tree, push to frontend
- Debounce directory changes (same 100ms as file changes)
- Add UserEvent::TreeChanged(tree_json) variant
- Frontend receives new tree and updates sidebar without losing expanded state

## Verification
- \`cargo build\` compiles without errors
- Create a new file in the directory → appears in sidebar
- Delete a file → disappears from sidebar
- Rename a file → updates in sidebar
- Expanded/collapsed state preserved on tree update
close_reason: Sidebar file tree implemented: TreeNode/FileType in files.rs, FileTree.svelte recursive component, file tree passed through init payload

title: Validate: Sidebar file tree
description:

title: Tab management for multiple files
description:
Each \`attn <path>\` call adds a tab. Tabs track their own file, scroll position, and edit state.

## Scope
- Tab bar UI at top of content area
- Keyboard shortcuts for tab management (Cmd+W, Cmd+[, Cmd+])
- Per-tab state: file path, scroll position, edit state, file watcher
- Sidebar click opens in current tab, Cmd+click for new tab
- Close last tab → daemon exits

## Pre-conditions
- Sidebar working (Feature: Sidebar with recursive file tree)
- Daemon working (Feature: Single-instance daemon)

title: Implement tab state management
description:

- web/src/lib/tabs.ts (new — tab state store)
- web/src/lib/types.ts (add Tab interface)
- web/src/App.svelte (wire tabs into app)

## Approach
- Tab interface: { id, path, fileType, scrollY, editState, label }
- Svelte store/state for: tabs array, activeTabId
- addTab(path): create tab, set active
- closeTab(id): remove tab, activate adjacent, exit if last
- switchTab(id): save current scroll position, restore target's
- IPC: when daemon receives new path via socket → call addTab

## Verification

title: Multi-type file viewers
description:
Support viewing images, video, and audio files alongside markdown. File type detection by extension
in Rust, with dedicated viewer components in Svelte.

## Scope
- ImageViewer.svelte: centered image, scroll/pinch to zoom
- MediaPlayer.svelte: HTML5 video/audio with native controls
- Gallery navigation: left/right arrows cycle through all supported files in directory
- File type detection in Rust (by extension) — send type + content/path to frontend
- Router in Svelte: based on file type, render correct viewer component

## Supported Types
| Type | Extensions | Viewer |
|------|-----------|--------|
| Images | .png, .jpg, .jpeg, .gif, .svg, .webp | Gallery viewer with zoom |
| Video | .mp4, .webm, .mov | HTML5 video player |
| Audio | .mp3, .wav, .ogg, .m4a | HTML5 audio player |

## Plan Reference
- planning/v2-plan.md (Phase 6)
close_reason: ImageViewer, MediaPlayer, viewer routing, and gallery navigation implemented

- ProseMirror plugin that watches for code_block nodes

- Use highlight.js to tokenize, create ProseMirror Decorations with colored spans

title: Fix mixed task-list conversion causing schema-invalid parses
description: Markdown list token conversion can produce a task_list containing non-task_list_item children when checkbox and non-checkbox items are mixed, which can throw parse errors and force safe mode.
acceptance_criteria: Mixed checkbox/non-checkbox lists never generate schema-invalid token streams.\nParser does not fall back to safe mode for mixed lists that should render normally.\nAdd regression tests covering mixed list cases.
close_reason: Task list conversion now only promotes fully task-item bullet lists, preventing schema-invalid mixed-list parses.

title: Add macOS bundle metadata and icon assets for attn
description: Add Cargo bundle metadata and icon configuration for macOS .app packaging. Include icon asset pipeline and project structure expected by cargo-bundle.

close_reason:
Added macOS cargo-bundle metadata, generated/checked in temporary placeholder icon assets, and validated ==> Building production app bundle for aarch64-apple-darwin
        /Users/jameslal/workspace/jl/attn/target/aarch64-apple-darwin/release/bundle/osx/attn.app
Built app: target/aarch64-apple-darwin/release/bundle/osx/attn.app produces target/aarch64-apple-darwin/release/bundle/osx/attn.app.

title: Make Mermaid fullscreen scroll lock robust for multiple opens
description: Fullscreen modal sets body overflow hidden and unconditionally restores it on close, which can break when multiple modals overlap.
acceptance_criteria: Body scroll lock uses singleton or reference-counted strategy.\nClosing one modal does not unlock scroll if another is still open.\nLifecycle test covers multi-open/close behavior.
close_reason: Mermaid fullscreen body scroll lock now uses reference counting for multi-open robustness.

title: Integrate shadcn-svelte + Tailwind CSS v4
description: Replace hand-rolled CSS system with shadcn-svelte component library and Tailwind CSS v4. Replaces custom Sidebar, TabBar, and theme variables with shadcn composable components and oklch() theming. ProseMirror editor and custom viewers stay.
close_reason: shadcn-svelte + Tailwind CSS v4 fully integrated. Completed: install, theme migration, sidebar, tabs, command palette, prosemirror styles, toast notifications, tooltip/scroll-area components installed.

title: Add GitHub Actions signing smoke-test workflow
description: Create dedicated workflow to verify certificate import and codesign process against a tiny test binary, mirroring gpu-cli-two test-signing pattern.
acceptance_criteria: - workflow runs manually\n- cert import and identity listing steps pass\n- signed test binary verification output included
close_reason: Added .github/workflows/test-signing.yml smoke test for certificate import, identity listing, codesign invocation, verification, and keychain cleanup.

title: Hide empty directories in sidebar tree
description: Sidebar currently includes directories even when they contain no previewable files. This creates noise and dead-end navigation.
acceptance_criteria: Given a project where a directory tree contains no markdown/image/video/audio files, that directory is not shown in the sidebar tree.\nGiven watcher tree upserts/patches, empty directories are still excluded and do not reappear.\nExisting previewable-file directories continue to render normally.
close_reason: Filtered empty directories from snapshot/upsert tree generation.

title: Fix typography and minimalism polish issues
description: Multiple small CSS issues identified from screenshot review: blockquote spacing too generous, italic conflicts with bold labels, faint border, noisy inline code borders, duplicate CSS blocks, content padding too tight
close_reason: Fixed 8 typography/minimalism issues: tightened blockquote paragraph spacing, removed conflicting italic, strengthened blockquote border, removed noisy inline code border, reduced inline code padding, increased content horizontal padding, reduced paragraph margins, cleaned duplicate CSS

title: Add GitHub Actions workflow for macOS desktop build + optional notarization
description: Create workflow similar to gpu-cli-two desktop-test: build mac artifact, import cert to temp keychain, sign, optionally notarize, staple, and upload artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: - workflow_dispatch supports notarize toggle\n- keychain import/cleanup included\n- signed/notarized artifact uploaded
close_reason: Added .github/workflows/macos-desktop-test.yml to build bundle artifacts and optionally perform signing + notarization, including keychain import and cleanup.

title: Enable ProseMirror table editing plugins in editor
description: Table plugins are defined but not installed in editor plugin composition, so expected table behaviors are missing.
acceptance_criteria: Editor installs table plugins (including navigation/editing behavior).\nTab/Shift-Tab table navigation works.\nRegression test(s) verify plugin stack includes table behavior.
close_reason: Editor now installs table plugins for table navigation/editing behavior.

title: Replace custom Sidebar with shadcn Sidebar
description:
Replace FileTree.svelte + Sidebar.svelte with shadcn Sidebar component. Use Sidebar.Provider, Sidebar.Root, Sidebar.Content, Sidebar.Group, Sidebar.Menu, Sidebar.MenuItem. Add collapsible support (Cmd+B), rail mode (icon-only collapsed state). Keep recursive file tree logic.

Files: web/src/lib/Sidebar.svelte (rewrite), web/src/lib/FileTree.svelte (rewrite), web/src/App.svelte (update layout)

Acceptance:
- File tree renders with expand/collapse
- Active file highlighted
- Cmd+B toggles sidebar collapse
- Rail mode shows icons only when collapsed
- Click file navigates, Cmd+click opens new tab
close_reason: Sidebar replaced with shadcn Sidebar + Collapsible by worker-sidebar
